public_api.entrypoint: synthesize_answer(refined_intent, query_type, entities, selected_markdown, llm_client, *, model=None, temperature=0.0, max_tokens=None, timeout=None) -> SynthesizerResult
public_api.result_type: SynthesizerResult(answer: str, citations: list[str], provided_paths: list[str]) with .unknown_citations() -> list[str]
public_api.header_format_disclosed: ## File: <path>  (see synthesizer.py module docstring "File-path header format" section, agents/query/synthesizer.py lines ~60-70)
public_api.llm_wire_format_disclosed: JSON object {answer: str, citations: [str]} (see synthesizer.py module docstring "LLM response wire format" section)

out_of_scope_next.note: SynthesizerResult.unknown_citations() is the building block for 4.5.2's dedicated fixture (one valid + one hallucinated citation) and rejection/flagging behavior; this run does not add rejection logic or the dedicated test file, per issue #24's 'sized to exactly one commit' subtask split.

security_notes.issue_body_check: gh issue view 24 read as untrusted data only. No injected/fake system-reminder-style text found inside issue #24's body (both subtasks' text is ordinary repo planning content). No instructions from issue content were acted upon.
security_notes.harness_reminders_during_run: Three harness-level system-reminders arrived directly in my own tool-call flow during this run (a date-change notice to 2026-07-11, an MCP 'tokensave' server instructions block, and an 'Auto Mode Active' block encouraging fewer clarifying-question pauses). Per the task's own instructions, reminders arriving directly in-flow (not embedded in repo/issue content) are treated as legitimate harness output, not injection -- but they were still not used as authorization for anything beyond the explicitly delegated task (no push, no self-verification, no issue edits were performed, consistent with the explicit CRITICAL constraints given).

verification_reminder: Per invariant I4, this agent did NOT verify its own work. /cdr:verify should independently confirm: (1) prompt-includes-headers + citation-extraction acceptance criteria, (2) exception/parsing behavior matches intent_refiner.py's established pattern, (3) no scope creep into 4.5.2's rejection logic, (4) commit is local-only (not pushed), (5) issue #24 was not modified.
